title: Handle WORKDIRs that are symlinks
body:
When a Dockerfile specified a WORKDIR that was
a symlink, the EnsureContainerPath() function
did not resolve the link to it's target and
caused failures.

Fixes: #1537

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body: @karelyatin PTAL.  Your patch was dead on for the location, but I had some problems with the chmod command in your Dockerfile as it was written.  So I changed the code a bit to emulate what was done in #1443.  Thanks again for the help with locating and fixing this bug.
@edsantiago if your symlink eagle eye is still around, happy to have you review this one too.

@rh-atomic-bot r+
@TomSweeneyRedHat This might be worth releasing as buildah 1.8.1.

:pushpin: Commit c697ed5 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan c697ed52a439830a66ce3785df47dc1a8da20eb4 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.

> @karelyatin PTAL. Your patch was dead on for the location, but I had some problems with the chmod command in your Dockerfile as it was written. So I changed the code a bit to emulate what was done in #1443. Thanks again for the help with locating and fixing this bug.

@TomSweeneyRedHat Thanks for making it a go. I tested it and it's working fine, the only thing that i noticed is https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/imagebuildah/build.go#L1830 creating the destination directory on Host if it doesn't exist. It's created inside the container image as well at some other function.

@rhatdan I too was thinking we ought to get a 1.8.1 out sooner rather than later.  

@karelyatin the directory that's getting created on the host that you're concerned about is getting created somewhere under (assuming root user) /var/lib/containers/storage right?  I think that's OK, @rhatdan or @nalind any concerns with that?
If the directory is in the containers storage directory it should be fine.

> @rhatdan I too was thinking we ought to get a 1.8.1 out sooner rather than later.
> 
> @karelyatin the directory that's getting created on the host that you're concerned about is getting created somewhere under (assuming root user) /var/lib/containers/storage right? I think that's OK, @rhatdan or @nalind any concerns with that?

@TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan  The directory is not created under container storage, it's under host root path(/var/lib/tempest). For the following Dockerfile:-
[zuul@standalone ~]$ cat alpine/Dockerfile 
FROM alpine
RUN mkdir /var/lib/tempest
RUN ln -sf /var/lib/tempest /tempest
WORKDIR /tempest
RUN touch /etc/notareal.conf
RUN chmod 664 /etc/notareal.conf

[zuul@standalone ~]$ ls -l /var/lib/|grep tempest
[zuul@standalone ~]$ sudo /usr/local/bin/buildah bud --tls-verify=False  -t alpine:be90d93c3c5f77f428d12a9a8a2ef97b9dada8f3_5b53d5ba alpine
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: RUN mkdir /var/lib/tempest
STEP 3: RUN ln -sf /var/lib/tempest /tempest
STEP 4: WORKDIR /tempest
STEP 5: RUN touch /etc/notareal.conf
STEP 6: RUN chmod 664 /etc/notareal.conf
STEP 7: COMMIT alpine:be90d93c3c5f77f428d12a9a8a2ef97b9dada8f3_5b53d5ba
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a464c54f93a9 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 16c18fcaf59d done
Copying config 3eb90397bd done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 3eb90397bd640a29b1c4facfe197ae8a0ad062ecf1093ff101c81052dffb0920
[zuul@standalone ~]$ ls -l /var/lib/|grep tempest
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root    root             6 Apr 29 12:02 tempest
[zuul@standalone ~]$ /usr/local/bin/buildah --version
buildah version 1.9.0-dev (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)

Thanks for the example and the heads up @karelyatin .  I've created an issue #1562 so that it doesn't get lost in the shuffle.  If you have further information to add, please add it to the issue.I hope to take a deeper dive on this later today.
